$Comment: Round-8 (facing-relative custody display + anvil weapon pose, 2026-07-23): Custody.Display Offset/Rotation are now FACING-RELATIVE (relative to the placed block's own yaw), not absolute world axes. RPG Stations' StationCustodyDisplay reads the block's non-deprecated getBlockRotationIndex yaw at spawn, rotates the horizontal Offset (X/Z) by it, and adds it into Rotation.Y, so a rotated anvil carries its weapon's position AND facing around with it. Convention: authored +Z = toward the block's FRONT, +X = its right; Y stays vertical; a DEFAULT-orientation placement (block yaw 0) leaves the block frame equal to the world frame, so every value here reads exactly as the pre-round-8 world-space authoring did (no blind re-tune needed). Round-8 step-synced puppet swings (RPG Stations engine change, same round): the enhance ritual's strike1 and strike2 steps now author a per-step Puppet.Clip of MMO_Emote_Hammer so the skinned puppet visibly HAMMERS on both strike beats, synced to each step's iteration entry (previously the puppet played its engage loop once and then stood still through both strikes). MMO_Emote_Hammer is the exact clip RPG Stations' generic swing route already resolves for the held hammer here (StationPuppetController.resolveEffectiveClip falls back to the resolved action's Animation.EmoteId, which the enhance action inherits from the station-level Animation.EmoteId = MMO_Emote_Hammer), authored explicitly per step. Because these steps author a Clip, the engine SUPPRESSES the generic engage/swing puppet clip for the whole enhance program (the step-entry clips are the sole animation driver, no double-fire); a stepped program with no step clips keeps its one generic engage swing. The settle step authors NO Puppet.Clip on purpose (the puppet idles for the settling pause), and the stamp step keeps its existing Puppet.Prop.Source None empty-hands override (composes cleanly - it authors a Prop but no Clip, so no hammer swing fires on the enchant-flourish beat). enhance.Custody.Display re-tuned for the maintainer's placed-weapon screenshot (weapon overhanging one edge of the anvil top, hilt/crossguard rolled upward): (a) Offset.X: 0.3 ADDED to pull the weapon back toward the anvil-top center - a facing-relative sideways shift matching the screenshot's leftward overhang. First guess ~0.25-0.35; the SIGN and AXIS are the primary in-game tunables: if it moves the wrong way flip to -0.3; if the overhang is actually along the blade's LENGTH (front/back) rather than sideways, move the leaf to Offset.Z instead (same magnitude). (b) Rotation.Z: 90.0 (roll) ADDED to the existing X: 90.0 (pitch) so the hilt lies flat. Euler reasoning: the prop rotation is Rotation3f(pitch=X, yaw=Y, roll=Z) composed in the engine's intrinsic Y-X-Z order (yaw, then pitch, then roll); X: 90 tips the blade from vertical (+Y) onto the horizontal (pointing +Z in the block's local frame), the crossguard then reads rolled-up because the flat weapon rests on its edge, so Z: 90 rolls it about its own long axis to bring the broad face down / crossguard flat. First guess; if it over- or under-rotates try Z: 270 (i.e. -90) or Z: 180. Offset.Y stays 0.55 (height was not flagged). Round-8 re-check of the other two placed displays: convert.Custody.Display (placed ingot, Offset.Y 0.52) and the sawmill's placed log (Offset.Y -0.1) author ONLY a vertical Offset.Y with no horizontal shift and no Rotation, so the facing-relative change leaves them BYTE-IDENTICAL at any placement orientation - deliberately left unchanged. FALLBACK (unchanged from round-7) if pitch AND roll are visually IGNORED by the client's item-mesh renderer for a bare ItemComponent prop (the one thing server source cannot prove): remove the authored X/Z Rotation leaves (zero visual regression vs pre-round-7, weapon stays upright), KEEP the Rotation schema, and escalate (a lying-flat custom display block or a Blockbench prop model), deliberately out of scope. Round-7 fix (design raw/rpg-stations-round7-fix-design-2026-07-23.md, D-1, leg F): enhance.Custody.Display gains Rotation: {X: 90.0} - the reported defect was the placed weapon standing upright on the anvil face instead of lying flat. X is pitch (tips the mesh onto the horizontal), authored in DEGREES (never raw radians), applied via RpgStations' resolveRotationRadians -> Rotation3f(pitch,yaw,roll). Ordered round-8 in-game tuning ladder (server-owner tunable, no engine change needed for any of these): (1) start with {X:90.0} - expect the blade horizontal, lying on the anvil face; (2) if the weapon instead stands edge-on or spins about its own length (mesh local axes differ per model), swap to {Z:90.0} (roll), then try combos ({X:90,Z:90}); (3) tune Y (yaw) last, purely for horizontal alignment along the anvil's ridge - Y is world-space, not placement-compensated, so one global value is a compromise across NESW station placements; (4) FALLBACK if pitch AND roll are visually IGNORED by the client's item-mesh renderer for a bare ItemComponent prop (the one thing server-side source cannot prove): remove the authored X/Z leaves entirely (zero visual regression vs pre-round-7, the weapon stays upright exactly as before), KEEP the Rotation schema (correct and engine-verified at the wire level down to the packet), and report the finding upstream - a lying-flat custom display block or a Blockbench prop model are the escalation options, deliberately out of this round's scope. convert.Custody.Display and the sawmill's placed log stay deliberately UNROTATED (Rotation omitted): the sawmill log takes the BlockEntity route (renders the actual block model, no defect reported), and the placed ingot's upright ItemComponent-prop default was never flagged, so both are left as-is - a one-leaf {"Y": 90.0} data change is all a later re-tune would need. The anvil work station (design section 9.5, phase 2 leg E): two diegetic actions selected by held/placed item - Convert (sharpen a vanilla metal bar) and Enhance (the flagship Stamp-step ritual). Both gate on holding a hammer (Tool, station-level, shared). Reuses the vanilla Anvil bench model (Common/Blocks/Benches/Anvil.blockymodel) - no new art. Smoke round R5 (2026-07-22): convert.Custody.Display.Offset.Y lowered from 0.55 to 0.35 so the placed ingot rests closer to the anvil surface - a maintainer-tunable FIRST GUESS from this round, not yet re-verified in-game (the maintainer flagged only the placed INGOT; enhance's placed-weapon offset is left at 0.55 pending separate confirmation). Identity.Icon removed: StationService#blockItemIdAt already falls back to the anchor block's own BlockType id when Identity.Icon is absent/blank, and that id equals this station's own item id (RPG_Station_Anvil, filename = item id per repo convention) - so the summary-HUD crest already shows the anvil's own block icon with zero duplicated id to drift. R7 fix-round correction (2026-07-22): that claim was FALSE at engage time for the convert action - a BarsPlaced/WeaponPlaced custody block is a distinct generated-key BlockType (e.g. *RPG_Station_Anvil_BarsPlaced), so the old getId()-only fallback resolved the UNKNOWN placeholder icon, not this station's own. Fixed engine-side: StationService#blockItemIdAt now resolves via BlockType#getItem() (the block's containing Item asset), which walks correctly through a state variant back to the base RPG_Station_Anvil item id - the fallback's original zero-duplicated-id-to-drift intent now genuinely holds. See RPG Stations' station/CLAUDE.md for the full source trail; no authoring change needed here. Work-start deny fix round (2026-07-22): the anvil denied every work-start attempt with ui.station.mount_unavailable (StationService#toggle's Entity-mount engage path denies with that exact toast whenever spawnAnchor/attach returns null/false) even holding the correct hammer, with the tool gate and custody viability already passed - StationEntityMountController's standing Entity-surface mount (design 9.2) is a phase-2 spike never verified in-game. Source reading of that same mechanism surfaced a SEPARATE, confirmed defect independent of whichever condition triggered the observed deny: the anchor entity never carried a NetworkId component, so PlayerMount/TrackerUpdate silently no-op'd against it - even a mount that DID succeed would have rendered invisibly. Hold.Mount moved to effect-mode (MovementLock + EffectId, the proven phase-1 default) rather than continuing to chase the Entity-mount spike. The engine-side NetworkId fix (StationEntityMountController) and graceful degradation (StationService#toggle now falls back to effect-mode instead of denying on a spawn/attach failure) both landed alongside this authoring change and hold regardless of which mount surface a future pack revision picks; this is the maintainer-recommended proven-hold swap, not a permanent design reversal - see additional-mods/rpg-stations station/CLAUDE.md for the full fix-round detail. Round-4 re-tune (2026-07-22): convert.Custody.Display.Offset.Y raised from 0.35 to 0.52 (maintainer-chosen) - R5's 0.35 sat the placed ingot too deep into the anvil surface; enhance's placed-weapon offset stays at 0.55, still pending its own separate confirmation. Round-4 puppet-presentation content leg (2026-07-22): authored ONE station-level Puppet group (Enabled true, Hide.Route Scale, the maintainer-crowned default; Look.Source PlayerClone; Prop.Source MirrorHeld so the puppet carries a copy of the held hammer) shared by BOTH convert and enhance - neither ActionDef overrides it, since both actions work the same anvil face and no distinct positioning is warranted; Offset/Yaw are a first-pass placement guess (world-space, kept small, same caveat as Custody.Display) pending the P7 smoke round. The enhance ritual's own stamp step DOES warrant a per-step override: Puppet.Prop.Source None on that one beat, so the puppet's hands go empty for the enchant flourish instead of still gripping the hammer mid-stamp. Positioning round-2 (2026-07-23): the sawmill's own Puppet group needed a fix round after its first in-game smoke (wrong facing, standing too close - see Sawmill.json's own $Comment) that this station's Puppet group had never independently received (it shipped in the SAME round-4 leg but was flagged 'not yet independently smoke-tested'). Since this station resolves its Puppet Offset/Yaw through the identical mechanism (StationPuppetController#spawnAndHide -> ziggfreed-common's PlayerPuppetService#offsetPosition/#spawn - a plain world-space anchor-plus-offset addition and a direct Rotation3f with no BlockMountPoint-style engine rotation on this path, same as the sawmill) and reuses a vanilla bench block the same way the sawmill does, this leg applies the SAME mirrored correction pre-emptively rather than waiting for a separate anvil-specific smoke round to rediscover the identical defect: Yaw flipped 180 from its prior guess (180.0 -> 0.0, matching the sawmill's own P-2 fix) and Offset.Z stepped back to 1.0 (matching the sawmill's now-twice-corrected final value, 0.4 -> 1.0) - both maintainer-tunable, PENDING the anvil's own in-game confirm (this station has never been independently puppet-smoked; the sawmill's proven fix pattern is a reasoned starting point, not a verified result for this block). The enhance ritual's stamp-step Puppet.Prop.Source override is untouched and keeps composing on top (per-step overrides only ever touch Clip/Prop, never Offset/Yaw).
